title: Area of rectangles with partial arrays
description: Find the area of rectangles when not all square units are shown.
topic_path: Topic B: Concepts of area measurement
Area of rectangles with partial arrays

assessment_items.question_content:
The following rectangle is partially split into unit squares.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the rectangle above?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] square units
hints:
Area is the number of square units that cover a shape.

Let's imagine that we finish drawing the unit squares in the rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

The rectangle has $\blueE{5}$ rows with $\maroonD{4}$ unit squares per row.
$\underbrace{\maroonD{4}+\maroonD{4}+\maroonD{4}+\maroonD{4}+\maroonD{4}}_{\blueE{5}\text{ rows}}= \goldE{20}$
$\text{Area }= \goldE{20} \text{ square units}$

options.alt: A rectangle divided by a partially complete grid of unit squares with 5 rows and 4 columns. Part of the rectangle is divided into 4 equal-sized squares. The remainder of the rectangle shows partially drawn rows and columns.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following rectangle is partially covered with unit squares.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which figure has the same area as the rectangle above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Area is the number of square units that cover a shape.

We need to find a rectangle with the same area.

Let's imagine that we finish drawing the unit squares in the rectangle.

- How many rows of unit squares would there be? 

- How many unit squares would be in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]

The rectangle has $\blueE{9}$ rows with $\maroonD{6}$ unit squares per row.

We would use the same number of unit squares to cover this rectangle, so it would have the same area:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A rectangle divided by a partially complete grid of unit squares with 9 rows and 6 columns. Part of the rectangle is divided into 14 equal-sized squares. The remainder of the rectangle shows partially drawn rows and columns.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]] is $1$ unit square.

**What is the area of rectangle $A$?**

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] square units
hints:
Area is the number of square units that cover a shape.

We can't see the square units to count them, but we can see how many are in each row. 

Let's add up the square units from all of the rows.

There are $\tealE{6}$ rows of $\goldE{4}$ square units each. 

[[☃ image 1]]
$\underbrace{\goldE{4}+\goldE{4}+\goldE{4}+\goldE{4}+\goldE{4}+\goldE{4}}_{\tealE{6}\text{ rows}}=\maroonD{24}$
$\text{Area }= \maroonD{24} \text{ square units}$

options.alt: A square.
A rectangle labeled Rectangle A covers 6 rows and 4 columns of the grid.
